New for M4: GraphLayout (12) + LanePalette shared in that suite + GraphRowRefs (7) + GraphCellUnit (3) + GraphRowContentUnit (9) + GraphModel (10) + GraphCellSnapshot (8 XCTest) + GraphRowContentSnapshot (8 XCTest) + GraphViewSnapshot (5 XCTest). KiteUITests NOT run (TCC blocker).

swiftformat.observation: swiftformat Sources Tests --lint: 0/97 files require formatting.

snapshotMd5Audit.observation: M4 snapshot suites are all internally md5-distinct: GraphCellSnapshotTests 8/8, GraphRowContentSnapshotTests 8/8, GraphViewSnapshotTests 5/5. Dark counterparts differ from light (MergeInbound-dark ebb53c95 vs light 9d35fea0, ShallowBanner-dark 72f663a2 vs light c15bc11f, CommitLimitFooter-dark b01e27f5 vs light 2421d939, GraphRowContent-dark fe27419e). Pre-existing dupes in RepoSidebarSnapshotTests (47fe1945×2, b519ca7d×2) and SettingsRootsTabSnapshotTests (d10d012d×4) REMAIN, both on the skip-list and owned by M8-fix-snapshot-degeneracy — NOT regressed by M4.

nonBlockingIssues.description: consumeConvergingLanes always inserts the commit's own column at the head of returned list and therefore always emits a self-column in-edge. Intentional (it draws the top-stub into the dot) but the variable name reads as if that column is 'converging'. Rename to incomingColumns + one-line comment distinguishing dot-entry stub from true converging lanes.
Force-unwrap on `LaneColor(rawValue: Int(slot))!`; invariant holds but `LaneColor.allCases[Int(slot)]` is equally cheap and safer if cases are ever marked non-contiguous.

Dot fallback uses LanePalette.color(for: commit.sha) for leaf rows (no out-edges) — SHA-seeded rather than branch-seeded. Leaf dots can visually clash with their descendants' branch color. Tracks the M4-graph-layout handoff's "branch-name-driven coloring" deferred follow-up.

Ownership crossover: file lives in Sources/Git/Layout/ (git-engine-worker territory per INTERFACES.md §3) but was written by the swiftui-worker per the feature brief. Reclassify in INTERFACES.md or move under a cross-archetype subfolder.
Per-row TimelineView(.periodic ... by: 60) — 200 rows = 200 scheduled re-renders/min. Pushing to an app-level minute broadcaster scales better when M7 diff pane adds more TimelineView consumers.

onChange(of: focus.lastChangeAt) spawns an unstructured Task per tick; activeReload cancels the INNER reload but the outer Task is not tracked. Same pattern flagged for BranchListView + StatusHeaderView in branch-list scrutiny. This is the 3rd per-focus observer; INTERFACES.md §4 says consolidate before the 4th. STRONGLY recommend a RepoDetailModel consolidation before M7-commit-diff lands.

Git.run reads `git log --all --topo-order -n 200 -z` output via readToEnd() — ~38 KB typical, could push past ~64 KB pipe-buffer boundary with pathological long subjects. Foundation scrutiny queued this as M1-fix-git-run-drain (pre-M7). Recommend prioritizing that fix ahead of M5 so graph reload can't block on extreme repos.
Transient reload failure wipes `rows = []`, emptying the List. Consider preserving prior rows + surfacing a banner so a brief flake doesn't cause a full view blank-out.

Empty-repo detection relies on stderr substring matching of English git messages. Documented assumption: Process env sets LC_ALL=C (AGENTS.md §4). Worth an inline comment at the matcher so a future localization reviewer doesn't try to internationalize it.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Upgrade §4 Shared state rules → Per-focus observer fan-out from 'before adding a 4th, consolidate' to 'before M5-fetch ships, consolidate'. M4 is the 3rd observer; by the time M7's two diff models land we're at 5 unstructured per-tick Tasks and the cost of the late refactor compounds. Propose: an @Observable RepoDetailModel on RepoFocus that exposes branchList + statusHeader + graph + uncommittedDiff + commitDiff as child @Observable fields, with a single `reloadAll(tick:)` method called from the RepoFocus-level task handle. Move the .onChange(of: focus.lastChangeAt) site from three views into one.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: M4 landed without consolidating. M5 net-ops will trigger fetch→reload cycles that hit all three observers simultaneously, amplifying the reload-storm effect. The refactor gets cheaper the sooner it lands.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Under Known gotchas → Git CLI, hoist the Git.run 64KB note: 'M4-graph-scroll-container is the first real consumer reading >10KB from Git.run (log of 200 commits, ~38KB typical). This is still under the ~64KB pipe-buffer boundary, but narrowing the margin. Any new large-output caller (M7 diff pane is the next one) MUST switch to Git.stream or the M1-fix-git-run-drain refactor must ship first. Reviewers: check for a new `Git.run(["log", ...])` OR `Git.run(["diff", ...])` OR `Git.run(["show", ...])` in any PR and flag.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: The foundation scrutiny flagged this as pre-M7; M4 narrowed the margin. The guidance needs to stay visible as new callers appear.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Under §3 File ownership, add a note: 'Sources/Git/Layout/ houses both the pure GraphLayout algorithm (git-engine-worker) AND the post-layout GraphRowRefs.enrich adapter (swiftui-worker). Either is acceptable; future workers touching Layout/ should check the per-file doc-comment for the originating feature before assuming archetype.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: M4-graph-row-meta's handoff flagged the crossover; codify rather than leave it as a per-worker surprise.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Add a note on reload-error state handling: 'A view model that fails a reload after a prior successful reload should prefer preserving the previous rendered state + surfacing a banner, not wiping state to empty. GraphModel's current behaviour (sets rows=[] on error) is the opposite — causes a full blank-out on transient flakes.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: M5 net-ops and M7 diff will both have transient network / disk failures that should not blank the UI.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Codify the 'fan-out inside queue boundary' subprocess discipline demonstrated by GraphModel.performReload: `await focus.queue.run { async let a = Git.run(...); async let b = Git.run(...); ...; return try await (a, b, ...) }`. The queue boundary is outer — guaranteeing per-repo serial access across ops. The fan-out is inner — 4 subprocess spawns happen concurrently under the same queue turn. This is the correct pattern when a model needs multiple reads from the same focused repo.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: BranchListModel (M3) does 3 sequential focus.queue.run calls for its detached-HEAD probe — same antipattern M4 flagged. GraphModel demonstrates the fix; codify so M5/M7 reach for the correct pattern first.

anyOrphansOrFailures.note: 60fps scroll performance is architecturally plausible (per-row Canvas, bounded segment count, virtualized List) but can only be verified via Instruments Core Animation capture on a real display. No unit / snapshot test covers it. Defer to user-testing-validator-graph on a TCC-unblocked host with Instruments.

selectedSHA selection state is tested (selectUpdatesState, clearEmptiesState, XCUITest testCommitTapSetsSelection authored). The 'opens diff viewer' half of the assertion requires M7-commit-diff — correctly scoped forward per features.json.

Stable Identifiable.id (LayoutRow.id == commit.sha) provides the scroll-preservation mechanism via SwiftUI's List diffing. The authored XCUITest testGraphRendersAfterFocus covers render-after-focus but not the scroll-position invariant across FSEvents refresh. Evidence gap closes on a TCC host running a FSEvents-synthesis test (fixture commit-while-scrolled).

carryOverObservations.note: Git.run 64KB pipe-buffer (M4 narrowed the margin to ~38KB; still under), Git.run cancel race, GitError.remoteRejected missing (M5 blocker), DiffParser quoted-path (M7), ProgressParser consume-all (M5), FSWatcher.start post-stop no-op, FSWatcher @unchecked-Sendable rationale missing, PersistenceStore silent swallows, setWindowFrame debounce, LogParser if-expr 5.10 dependency, RefKind.head case — M4 actually PRODUCES .head now via GraphRowRefs.prependHeadIfCurrent, resolving the foundation-era 'dead case' finding. Other items unchanged.

SettingsRootsTab & RepoSidebar degenerate snapshots remain on the skip-list; M4's own snapshots do NOT fall into the degenerate trap. xcodegen auto-resources + RepoSidebarModel double-trigger + DiscoveredRepo.id = URL all unchanged.

BranchListModel.lastFSTick is now READ (line 48) — partially remedies the dead-field flag; StatusHeaderModel.lastFSTick likewise read. RepoFocus.rootTask remains declared-but-unused (cancellation path only, never assigned). GitQueue @unchecked Sendable CompletionGate still lacks a justification doc-comment. BranchListModel.probeDetachedHead still fires 3 sequential focus.queue.run calls. Fan-out pattern across observers NOT consolidated in M4 — scheduled for pre-M7 refactor.

(1) Ownership crossover (GraphRowRefs.swift in git-engine territory, written by swiftui-worker); (2) 3rd per-focus observer without consolidation — tension with INTERFACES.md §4 prescription; (3) transient error wipes rows list; (4) Git.run log output approaches pipe-buffer boundary.

deferredUserTestingFollowUps: Run Tests/KiteUITests/GraphUITests.swift (4 cases: rendersAfterFocus, commitTapSetsSelection, shallowBannerAppears, commitLimitFooterAppears) on a TCC-unblocked host.
Capture Instruments Core Animation profile for a 200-commit repo scroll — verify ≥55fps (VAL-GRAPH-008).

Manually validate VAL-GRAPH-010 scroll-position preservation: open a ≥200-commit repo, scroll mid-list, run `git commit --allow-empty` externally, confirm scroll position is preserved after FSEvents refresh.
Visual review of octopus merge rendering against a real octopus-merge repo (e.g. Linux-kernel-ish subtree merges in a fixture).

requiredBeforeM5: Queue M1-fix-git-error-push-cases (new GitError cases for push — .remoteRejected / .protectedBranch / .hookRejected). M5-pull-push needs these or it'll be surfaced via a generic error toast.
strongRecommendBeforeM7: M1-fix-git-run-drain (refactor Git.run to drain pipes concurrently) — M7 diff pane WILL exceed 64KB on real commits. Close to blocking for M7 but not M5.
RepoDetailModel consolidation (per INTERFACES.md §4) — 3 observers today, 5 after M7. Every milestone's refactor cost grows.
